reasoning: The Manifold market was priced at 22% on June 12, 2025. The most recent CPI data for May 2025 (released June 11) showed a headline annual inflation rate of 2.4% and a core rate of 2.8%. Because the June 12 market price already fully incorporates the latest May CPI report, the 22% probability reflects a strong consensus that inflation will rebound above 2.5% by the end of the year. This is supported by professional forecasts—such as FactSet projecting December 2025 CPI at 2.6%—and economists' expectations that new tariffs imposed by the Trump administration will increasingly build upward price pressures throughout the rest of 2025. Given that 2.5% represents a very tight threshold and the tailwinds for inflation point upward, a forecast of 0.22 is well-supported.
